findings.evidence: grep: `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphicLine.lean:166:lemma associatedBilinForm_apply_apply_self_eq`; signature uses `{U}` and `(hF : IsComplexLinearMap J₀ J F₀) (v : U)`, with no `stdComplexLineReal`/`stdComplexLineImag`.
findings.fix: Move this lemma to an earlier general symplectic file, e.g. near `IsComplexLinearMap`/`SymplecticForm.associatedBilinForm` in `AlmostComplex.lean` or another general compatible-metric module, then import that from `JHolomorphicLine`/`JHolomorphicEnergy` if needed.
findings.issue: `IsComplexLinearMap.associatedBilinForm_apply_apply_self_eq` is generic in an arbitrary source `U` and does not use the standard complex line, so placing it in `JHolomorphicLine` hides reusable API behind a line-specific module.

findings.evidence: grep: `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphicLine.lean:185:lemma associatedBilinForm_apply_self_eq_symplecticForm`; the statement is for `(hF : IsComplexLinearMap J₀ J F₀) (v : U)`, while `JHolomorphicEnergy.lean:82` and `:92` are the actual standard-line specializations.
findings.fix: Move it with the preceding generic associated-bilinear-form lemma to a general symplectic/almost-complex module, leaving only the `stdComplexLine*` specializations in `JHolomorphicEnergy`.
findings.issue: `IsComplexLinearMap.associatedBilinForm_apply_self_eq_symplecticForm` is likewise generic for any complex source and belongs before the standard-line specialization layer.

summary: The standard-line energy declarations are in a natural downstream file, but the PR also adds generic complex-linear/associated-metric lemmas inside the standard-line module. Those generic lemmas should live with the general symplectic almost-complex API rather than forcing arbitrary complex-linear-map users to import `JHolomorphicLine`.
